title: Fragment of a list of names
support: A small triangular fragment of white marble, intact along the lower edge, but broken on the other sides. The reverse is cut flat, but only roughly finished.

layout.p: Preserves traces of 6 lines of Greek letters, with a clear vacat below. There are clear spaces between each word (name).

handNote.lettering: Moderately regular thinly incised letters with pronounced terminal serifs, and a fairly square module. Alpha with straight bar; delta (and sigma) show slight curvature; kappa with short arms; omicron slightly smaller (c.2mm less) and above the line; sigma with parallel outer strokes, and sometimes a pronounced extension to the mid-join; phi with extended vertical (11mm) and small over-semi-circular loops.

provenanceFound._: Purchased by the Siracusa Museum, as coming from Akrai, in 1888; now in storage, magazzino B, cass.6

museum.name: Museo Archeologico Regionale Paolo Orsi
museum.description: An outstanding collection of archaeological material. The collection ranges from Siracusa and the surrounding territory (Megara Hyblaia, Akrai, Kasmenai etc) to encompass all of eastern Sicily, reaching as far as Gela and Agrigento. The ground floor covers prehistory through to the end of the Classical period. Upstairs includes Hellenistic and Roman Syracuse and a display of material from the catacombs. A gallery displaying material from Hellenistic eastern Sicily is in preparation.
